_note: Huang Zhong kills Xiahou Yuan at Dingjun Mountain (March 219, key turning point of the Hanzhong campaign); Zhao Zilong's 'empty camp' on the Han River.
Zhuge Liang completes the Hanzhong campaign; Cao Cao's 'Chicken-rib' retreat from Ye Valley (May 219); Yang Xiu executed.
Liu Bei proclaimed King of Hanzhong (autumn 219); Guan Yu opens the Fancheng/Xiangyang campaign.
Pang De's coffin march; Guan Yu drowns Yu Jin's seven armies in the autumn floods (August 219, Sanguozhi).
Hua Tuo scrapes the poison from Guan Yu's bone; Lu Meng's 'white-robed merchants' surprise crossing seizes Jingzhou (autumn 219).
Xu Huang relieves Fancheng (October 219); Guan Yu, defections at Nanjun and Gongan in his rear, retreats to Maicheng.
Guan Yu captured and beheaded by Pan Zhang (December 219); Cao Cao haunted by his apparition at Luoyang as his own end draws near.
Hua Tuo executed by Cao Cao; Cao Cao dies at Luoyang (15 March 220, Sanguozhi); Cao Pi succeeds as King of Wei.
Cao Pi consolidates power, drives Cao Zhi to compose the Seven-Step Poem; text explicitly cites 'Prolonged Wealth Era, the First Year (AD 220)' and 'eighth month of that same year (AD 220)'.
Cao Pi deposes Emperor Xian (Han falls, December 220) and founds Wei; Liu Bei proclaimed Emperor of Shu-Han at Chengdu (May 221).
